{{infobox football tournament season
|title                      = Copa do Brasil
|year                       = 2018
|other_titles               =
|image                      = File:2018_Copa_do_Brasil_logo.jpg
|imagesize                  = 275 px
|caption                    = 
|country                    = Brazil
|dates                      = 30 January - 17 October
|num_teams                  = 91
|champions                  = [[Cruzeiro Esporte Clube|Cruzeiro]] {{small|(6th title)}}
|runner-up                  = [[Sport Club Corinthians Paulista|Corinthians]]
|continentalcup1            = [[2019 Copa Libertadores]]
|continentalcup1 qualifiers = [[Cruzeiro Esporte Clube|Cruzeiro]]
|matches                    = 120
|goals                      = 253
|top goal scorer            = [[Gabriel Barbosa|Gabriel]]<br>[[Neílton]]<br>[[Rômulo dos Santos de Souza|Rômulo]]<br>(4 goals each)
|player                     = [[Jádson]] (Corinthians)
|prevseason                 = [[2017 Copa do Brasil|2017]]
|nextseason                 = [[2019 Copa do Brasil|2019]]
}}

The '''2018 Copa do Brasil''' (officially the '''Copa [[Continental AG|Continental Pneus]] do Brasil 2018''' for sponsorship reasons)<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.meioemensagem.com.br/home/marketing/2016/01/08/continental-pneus-da-nome-a-copa-do-brasil.html |title=Continental Pneus patrocina Copa do Brasil |publisher=Meio & Mensagem |language=Portuguese |date=8 January 2016}}</ref> was the 30th edition of the [[Copa do Brasil]] football competition. It was held between 30 January and 17 October 2018. The competition was contested by 91 teams, which qualified either by participating in their respective state championships (70), by the 2018 [[CBF ranking]] (10), by the [[2017 Copa do Nordeste]] (1), by the [[2017 Copa Verde]] (1), by the [[2017 Campeonato Brasileiro Série B|2017 Série B]] (1) or by qualifying for the [[2018 Copa Libertadores]] (8).

[[Cruzeiro Esporte Clube|Cruzeiro]] were the defending champions. They successfully defended their title, winning the finals 3–1 on aggregate against [[Sport Club Corinthians Paulista|Corinthians]] for their 6th title.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.cbf.com.br/futebol-brasileiro/competicoes/copa-brasil-masculino/2018/120 |title=Cruzeiro vence o Corinthians e é campeão da Copa do Brasil 2018 |publisher=CBF |language=Portuguese |date=17 October 2018}}</ref> As champions, Cruzeiro qualified for the [[2019 Copa Libertadores]] Group stage and the [[2019 Copa do Brasil]] Round of 16.<ref name="regulations">{{cite web|url=https://cdn.cbf.com.br/content/201801/20180108150324_0.pdf |title=Regulamento Específico da Competição Copa do Brasil 2018 |publisher=CBF |language=Portuguese}}</ref>

[[Jádson]] (Corinthians) and [[Cássio Ramos|Cássio]] (Corinthians) won best player and best goalkeeper awards, respectively.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.torcedores.com/noticias/2018/10/jadson-vencedor-do-premio-de-luva-de-ouro-da-copa-do-brasil |title=Jadson é o vencedor do prêmio Bola de Ouro da Copa do Brasil de 2018 |publisher=Torcedores |language=Portuguese |date=17 October 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.torcedores.com/noticias/2018/10/cassio-vencedor-premio-luva-de-ouro-copa-do-brasil |title=Cássio é o vencedor do prêmio Luva de Ouro da Copa do Brasil de 2018 |publisher=Torcedores |language=Portuguese |date=17 October 2018}}</ref>

==Format==
The competition was a [[single elimination]] [[knockout tournament]], the first two stages featuring a single match and the other stages featuring [[two-legged tie]]s. Eleven teams qualified for the Round of 16 (the that teams qualified for the 2018 Copa Libertadores (8), Série B champions, Copa Verde champions and Copa do Nordeste champions). The remaining 80 teams played the first stage. The 40 winners played the second stage, the 20 winners played the third stage, the 10 winners played the fourth stage. The five fourth-stage winners qualified for the Round of 16.<ref name="regulations"/>

In this season, the [[away goals rule]] was not to be used in any stage.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.cbf.com.br/noticias/campeonato-copa-brasil-masculino/copa-do-brasil-2018-nao-tera-gol-qualificado |title=Copa do Brasil 2018 não terá gol qualificado |publisher=CBF |language=Portuguese |date=1 December 2017}}</ref>

==Qualified teams==
Teams in '''bold''' are qualified directly for the round of 16.

{| class="wikitable"
|-
!width=130|Association
!width=210|Team
!Qualification method
|-
|rowspan=2 |{{flagicon|Acre}} [[Federação de Futebol do Estado do Acre|Acre]]<br>{{small|2 berths}}
|[[Atlético Acreano]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Acriano]] champions
|-
|[[Rio Branco Football Club|Rio Branco]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Acriano]] runners-up
|-
|rowspan=3 |{{flagicon|Alagoas}} [[Federação Alagoana de Futebol|Alagoas]]<br>{{small|3 berths}}
|[[Clube de Regatas Brasil|CRB]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Alagoano]] champions
|-
|[[Centro Sportivo Alagoano|CSA]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Alagoano]] runners-up
|-
|[[Agremiação Sportiva Arapiraquense|ASA]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Alagoano]] 3rd place
|-
|{{flagicon|Amapá}} [[Federação Amapaense de Futebol|Amapá]]<br>{{small|1 berth}}
|[[Santos Futebol Clube (AP)|Santos]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Amapaense]] champions
|-
|rowspan=2 |{{flagicon|Amazonas}} [[Federação Amazonense de Futebol|Amazonas]]<br>{{small|2 berths}}
|[[Manaus Futebol Clube|Manaus]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Amazonense]] champions
|-
|[[Nacional Futebol Clube|Nacional]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Amazonense]] runners-up
|-
|rowspan=4 |{{flagicon|Bahia}} [[Federação Bahiana de Futebol|Bahia]]<br>{{small|3 + 1 berths}}
|'''[[Esporte Clube Bahia|Bahia]]'''
|'''[[2017 Copa do Nordeste]] champions'''
|-
|[[Esporte Clube Vitória|Vitória]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Baiano]] champions
|-
|[[Fluminense de Feira Futebol Clube|Fluminense de Feira]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Baiano]] 3rd place
|-
|[[Esporte Clube Primeiro Passo Vitória da Conquista|Vitória da Conquista]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Baiano]] 4th place
|-
|rowspan=3 |{{flagicon|Ceará}} [[Federação Cearense de Futebol|Ceará]]<br>{{small|3 berths}}
|[[Ceará Sporting Club|Ceará]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Cearense]] champions
|-
|[[Ferroviário Atlético Clube (CE)|Ferroviário]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Cearense]] runners-up
|-
|[[Floresta Esporte Clube|Floresta]]
|2017 [[Copa Fares Lopes]] champions
|-
|{{flagicon|Espírito Santo}} [[Federação de Futebol do Estado do Espírito Santo|Espírito Santo]]<br>{{small|1 berth}}
|[[Clube Atlético Itapemirim|Atlético Itapemirim]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Capixaba]] champions
|-
|rowspan=2 |{{flagicon|Distrito Federal}} [[Federação de Futebol do Distrito Federal|Federal District]]<br>{{small|2 berths}}
|[[Brasiliense F.C.|Brasiliense]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Brasiliense]] champions
|-
|[[Ceilândia Esporte Clube|Ceilândia]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Brasiliense]] runners-up
|-
|rowspan=4 |{{flagicon|Goiás}} [[Federação Goiana de Futebol|Goiás]]<br>{{small|3 + 1 berths}}
|[[Goiás Esporte Clube|Goiás]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Goiano]] champions
|-
|[[Vila Nova Futebol Clube|Vila Nova]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Goiano]] runners-up
|-
|[[Associação Atlética Aparecidense|Aparecidense]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Goiano]] 3rd place
|-
|[[Atlético Clube Goianiense|Atlético Goianiense]]
|2nd best placed team in the 2017 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|rowspan=2 |{{flagicon|Maranhão}} [[Federação Maranhense de Futebol|Maranhão]]<br>{{small|2 berths}}
|[[Sampaio Corrêa Futebol Clube|Sampaio Corrêa]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Maranhense]] champions
|-
|[[Cordino Esporte Clube|Cordino]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Maranhense]] runners-up 
|-
|rowspan=4 |{{flagicon|Mato Grosso}} [[Federação Mato-Grossense de Futebol|Mato Grosso]]<br>{{small|3 + 1 berths}}
|'''[[Luverdense Esporte Clube|Luverdense]]'''
|'''[[2017 Copa Verde]] champions'''
|-
|[[Cuiabá Esporte Clube|Cuiabá]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Mato-Grossense]] champions
|-
|[[Sinop Futebol Clube|Sinop]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Mato-Grossense]] runners-up
|-
|[[União Esporte Clube|União Rondonópolis]]{{ref label|id4|a|a}}
|2017 [[Copa FMF]] champions
|-
|rowspan=2 |{{flagicon|Mato Grosso do Sul}} [[Federação de Futebol de Mato Grosso do Sul|Mato Grosso do Sul]] {{small|2 berths}}
|[[Corumbaense Futebol Clube|Corumbaense]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Sul-Mato-Grossense]] champions
|-
|[[Novoperário Futebol Clube|Novoperário]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Sul-Mato-Grossense]] runners-up
|-
|rowspan=7 |{{flagicon| Minas Gerais}} [[Federação Mineira de Futebol|Minas Gerais]]<br>{{small|4 + 2 + 1 berths}}
|'''[[Cruzeiro Esporte Clube|Cruzeiro]]'''
|'''[[2017 Copa do Brasil]] champions'''
|-
|'''[[América Futebol Clube (MG)|América Mineiro]]'''
|'''[[2017 Campeonato Brasileiro Série B]] champions'''
|-
|[[Clube Atlético Mineiro|Atlético Mineiro]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Mineiro]] champions
|-
|[[União Recreativa dos Trabalhadores|URT]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Mineiro]] 4th place
|-
|[[Associação Atlética Caldense|Caldense]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Mineiro]] 5th place
|-
|[[Uberlândia Esporte Clube|Uberlândia]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Mineiro]] 6th place
|-
|[[Boa Esporte Clube|Boa Esporte]]
|9th best placed team in the 2017 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|rowspan=3 |{{flagicon|Pará}} [[Federação Paraense de Futebol|Pará]]<br>{{small|3 berths}}
|[[Paysandu Sport Club|Paysandu]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Paraense]] champions
|-
|[[Clube do Remo|Remo]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Paraense]] runners-up
|-
|[[Independente Atlético Clube (PA)|Independente]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Paraense]] 3rd place
|-
|rowspan=2 |{{flagicon|Paraíba}} [[Federação Paraibana de Futebol|Paraíba]]<br>{{small|2 berths}}
|[[Botafogo Futebol Clube (PB)|Botafogo]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Paraibano]] champions
|-
|[[Treze Futebol Clube|Treze]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Paraibano]] runners-up
|-
|rowspan=5 |{{flagicon|Paraná}} [[Federação Paranaense de Futebol|Paraná]]<br>{{small|3 + 2 berths}}
|[[Coritiba Foot Ball Club|Coritiba]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Paranaense]] champions
|-
|[[Clube Atlético Paranaense|Atlético Paranaense]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Paranaense]] runners-up
|-
|[[Cianorte Futebol Clube|Cianorte]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Paranaense]] 3rd place
|-
|[[Paraná Clube|Paraná]]
|3rd best placed team in the 2017 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|[[Londrina Esporte Clube|Londrina]]
|10th best placed team in the 2017 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|rowspan=4 |{{flagicon|Pernambuco}} [[Federação Pernambucana de Futebol|Pernambuco]]<br>{{small|3 + 1 berths}}
|[[Sport Club do Recife|Sport]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Pernambucano]] champions
|-
|[[Salgueiro Atlético Clube|Salgueiro]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Pernambucano]] runners-up
|-
|[[Santa Cruz Futebol Clube|Santa Cruz]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Pernambucano]] 3rd place
|-
|[[Clube Náutico Capibaribe|Náutico]]
|5th best placed team in the 2017 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|rowspan=2 |{{flagicon|Piauí}} [[Federação de Futebol do Piauí|Piauí]]<br>{{small|2 berths}}
|[[Associação Atlética de Altos|Altos]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Piauiense]] champions
|-
|[[Parnahyba Sport Club|Parnahyba]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Piauiense]] runners-up
|-
|rowspan=7 |{{flagicon|Rio de Janeiro}} [[Federação de Futebol do Estado do Rio de Janeiro|Rio de Janeiro]]<br>{{small|5 + 2 berths}}
|'''[[Clube de Regatas do Flamengo|Flamengo]]'''
|'''[[2017 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A]] 6th place'''
|-
|'''[[CR Vasco da Gama|Vasco da Gama]]'''
|'''[[2017 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A]] 7th place'''
|-
|[[Fluminense FC|Fluminense]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Carioca]] runners-up
|-
|[[Botafogo de Futebol e Regatas|Botafogo]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Carioca]] 4th place
|-
|[[Nova Iguaçu Futebol Clube|Nova Iguaçu]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Carioca]] 5th place
|-
|[[Madureira Esporte Clube|Madureira]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Carioca]] 6th place
|-
|[[Boavista Sport Club|Boavista]]
|2017 [[Copa Rio (state cup)|Copa Rio]] champions
|-
|rowspan=3 |{{nowrap|{{flagicon|Rio Grande do Norte}} [[Federação Norte-Riograndense de Futebol|Rio Grande do Norte]]}} {{small|3 berths}}
|[[ABC Futebol Clube|ABC]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Potiguar]] champions
|-
|[[Globo Futebol Clube|Globo]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Potiguar]] runners-up
|-
|[[América Futebol Clube (RN)|América de Natal]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Potiguar]] 3rd place
|-
|rowspan=6 |{{flagicon|Rio Grande do Sul}} [[Federação Gaúcha de Futebol|Rio Grande do Sul]]<br>{{small|4 + 1 + 1 berths}}
|'''[[Grêmio Foot-Ball Porto Alegrense|Grêmio]]'''
|'''[[2017 Copa Libertadores]] champions'''
|-
|[[Esporte Clube Novo Hamburgo|Novo Hamburgo]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Gaúcho]] champions
|-
|[[Sport Club Internacional|Internacional]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Gaúcho]] runners-up
|-
|[[Sociedade Esportiva e Recreativa Caxias do Sul|Caxias]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Gaúcho]] 3rd place
|-
|[[Clube Esportivo Aimoré|Aimoré]]
|2017 [[Copa FGF]] runners-up
|-
|[[Esporte Clube Juventude|Juventude]]
|6th best placed team in the 2017 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|{{flagicon|Rondônia}} [[Federação de Futebol do Estado de Rondônia|Rondônia]]<br>{{small|1 berth}}
|[[Real Desportivo Ariquemes Futebol Clube|Real Ariquemes]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Rondoniense]] champions
|-
|{{flagicon|Roraima}} [[Federação Roraimense de Futebol|Roraima]]<br>{{small|1 berth}}
|[[São Raimundo Esporte Clube (RR)|São Raimundo]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Roraimense]] champions
|-
|rowspan=7 |{{flagicon|Santa Catarina}} [[Federação Catarinense de Futebol|Santa Catarina]]<br>{{small|4 + 1 + 2 berths}}
|'''[[Associação Chapecoense de Futebol|Chapecoense]]'''
|'''[[2017 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A]] 8th place'''
|-
|[[Avaí FC|Avaí]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Catarinense]] runners-up
|-
|[[Criciúma Esporte Clube|Criciúma]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Catarinense]] 3rd place
|-
|[[Brusque Futebol Clube|Brusque]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Catarinense]] 4th place
|-
|[[Clube Atlético Tubarão|Tubarão]]
|2017 [[Copa Santa Catarina]] champions
|-
|[[Figueirense FC|Figueirense]]
|best placed team in the 2017 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|[[Joinville Esporte Clube|Joinville]]
|4th best placed team in the 2017 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|rowspan=10 |{{flagicon|São Paulo}} [[Federação Paulista de Futebol|São Paulo]]<br>{{small|5 + 3 + 2 berths}}
|'''[[Sport Club Corinthians Paulista|Corinthians]]'''
|'''[[2017 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A]] champions'''
|-
|'''[[Sociedade Esportiva Palmeiras|Palmeiras]]'''
|'''[[2017 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A]] runners-up'''
|-
|'''[[Santos FC|Santos]]'''
|'''[[2017 Campeonato Brasileiro Série A]] 3rd place'''
|-
|[[Associação Atlética Ponte Preta|Ponte Preta]]
| [[2017 Campeonato Paulista]] runners-up
|-
|[[São Paulo FC|São Paulo]]
|[[2017 Campeonato Paulista]] 4th place
|-
|[[Ituano FC|Ituano]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Paulista#Campeonato Paulista do Interior|Campeonato Paulista do Interior]] champions
|-
|[[Associação Desportiva São Caetano|São Caetano]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Paulista Série A2]] champions
|-
|[[Associação Atlética Internacional (Limeira)|Internacional de Limeira]]
|2017 [[Copa Paulista]] runners-up
|-
|[[Clube Atlético Bragantino|Bragantino]]
|7th best placed team in the 2017 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|[[Oeste Futebol Clube|Oeste]]
|8th best placed team in the 2017 [[CBF ranking]] not already qualified
|-
|rowspan=2 |{{flagicon|Sergipe}} [[Federação Sergipana de Futebol|Sergipe]]<br>{{small|2 berths}}
|[[Associação Desportiva Confiança|Confiança]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Sergipano]] champions
|-
|[[Associação Olímpica de Itabaiana|Itabaiana]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Sergipano]] runners-up
|-
|{{flagicon|Tocantins}} [[Federação Tocantinense de Futebol|Tocantins]]<br>{{small|1 berth}}
|[[Interporto Futebol Clube|Interporto]]
|2017 [[Campeonato Tocantinense]] champions
|}
:{{note label|id4||a}}{{small|Originally [[Clube Esportivo Dom Bosco|Dom Bosco]] qualified as 2017 Copa FMF runners-up. Two months after the ending of the Copa FMF, Superior Tribunal de Justiça Desportiva (STJD) gave back 9 points deducted to União Rondonópolis in the first stage. With the 9 points, União Rondonópolis finished fourth and qualified for the 2017 Copa FMF semi-finals instead of Mixto. The previous semi-finals, Dom Bosco v Mixto, were annulled and União Rondonópolis played new semi-finals against Dom Bosco.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.cbf.com.br/stjd/noticias/uniao-recupera-pontos-da-copa-fmf-2017 |title=União recupera pontos da Copa FMF 2017 |publisher=CBF |date=24 January 2018 |language=Portuguese |access-date=2 March 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180303050324/https://www.cbf.com.br/stjd/noticias/uniao-recupera-pontos-da-copa-fmf-2017 |archive-date=3 March 2018 |url-status=dead |df=dmy-all }}</ref> União Rondonópolis won on penalties and advanced to the finals earning the right to play in the 2018 Copa do Brasil.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://globoesporte.globo.com/mt/noticia/uniao-perde-para-dom-bosco-mas-garante-vaga-na-copa-do-brasil-nos-penaltis.ghtml |title=União perde para Dom Bosco, mas garante vaga na Copa do Brasil nos pênaltis |publisher=Globo |date=3 February 2018 |language=Portuguese}}</ref>}}

==Schedule==
The schedule of the competition is as follows.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://cdn.cbf.com.br/content/201712/20171201195110_0.pdf |title=Copa do Brasil - Tabela Básica / Edição 2018 |publisher=CBF |language=Portuguese}}</ref>

{| class="wikitable"
|-
!Stage
!First leg
!Second leg
|-
|First stage
|colspan=2|{{unbulleted list
|Week 1: 31 January 2018
|Week 2: 7 February 2018
}}
|-
|Second stage
|colspan=2|{{unbulleted list
|Week 1: 14 February 2018
|Week 2: 21 February 2018
}}
|-
|Third stage
|28 February 2018
|14 March 2018
|-
|Fourth stage
|4 and 11 April 2018
|18 April 2018
|-
|Round of 16
|25 April, 2, 9 and 16 May 2018
|9, 16, 23 May and 16 July 2018
|-
|Quarterfinals
|1 August 2018
|15 August 2018
|-
|Semifinals
|12 September 2018
|26 September 2018
|-
|Finals
|10 October 2018
|17 October 2018
|}

==Draw==
{{#lst:2018 Copa do Brasil First Stage|Draw}}
{{clear}}

==First stage==
{{main|2018 Copa do Brasil First Stage#First Stage|l1=2018 Copa do Brasil first stage}}
{{#lst:2018 Copa do Brasil First Stage|Format}}
{{#lst:2018 Copa do Brasil First Stage|First stage}}

==Second stage==
{{main|2018 Copa do Brasil Second Stage#Second Stage|l1=2018 Copa do Brasil second stage}}
{{#lst:2018 Copa do Brasil Second Stage|Format}}
{{#lst:2018 Copa do Brasil Second Stage|Second stage}}

==Third stage==
{{main|2018 Copa do Brasil Third Stage#Third Stage|l1=2018 Copa do Brasil third stage}}
{{#lst:2018 Copa do Brasil Third Stage|Format}}
{{#lst:2018 Copa do Brasil Third Stage|Third stage}}

==Fourth stage==
{{main|2018 Copa do Brasil Fourth Stage#Fourth Stage|l1=2018 Copa do Brasil fourth stage}}
{{#lst:2018 Copa do Brasil Fourth Stage|Draw2}}
{{#lst:2018 Copa do Brasil Fourth Stage|Format}}
{{#lst:2018 Copa do Brasil Fourth Stage|Fourth stage}}

==Final stages==
{{main|2018 Copa do Brasil Final Stages#Final Stages|l1=2018 Copa do Brasil final stages}}
{{#lst:2018 Copa do Brasil Final Stages|Format}}

===Bracket===
{{#lst:2018 Copa do Brasil Final Stages|Bracket}}

===Round of 16===
{{#lst:2018 Copa do Brasil Final Stages|Draw3}}
{{#lst:2018 Copa do Brasil Final Stages|Round of 16}}

===Quarter-finals===
{{#lst:2018 Copa do Brasil Final Stages|Draw4}}
{{#lst:2018 Copa do Brasil Final Stages|Quarterfinals}}

===Semi-finals===
{{#lst:2018 Copa do Brasil Final Stages|Draw5}}
{{#lst:2018 Copa do Brasil Final Stages|Semifinals}}

===Finals===
{{main|2018 Copa do Brasil Finals}}
{{#lst:2018 Copa do Brasil Final Stages|Draw6}}
{{#lst:2018 Copa do Brasil Final Stages|Matches}}
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center; margin: 0 auto;"
|-
!2018 Copa do Brasil Champions
|-
|'''[[Cruzeiro Esporte Clube|Cruzeiro]]'''<br>'''6th Title'''
|}

==Top goalscorers==
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
|-
!Rank
!Player
!Team
!Goals
|-
|rowspan=3|1
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Gabriel Barbosa|Gabriel]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|São Paulo}} [[Santos FC|Santos]]
|rowspan=3|4
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Neílton]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Bahia}} [[Esporte Clube Vitória|Vitória]]
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Rômulo dos Santos de Souza|Rômulo]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Santa Catarina}} [[Avaí FC|Avaí]]
|-
|rowspan=8|4
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Denílson Pereira Júnior|Denílson]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Bahia}} [[Esporte Clube Vitória|Vitória]]
|rowspan=8|3
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Guilherme Milhomem Gusmão|Guilherme]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Paraná}} [[Clube Atlético Paranaense|Atlético Paranaense]]
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[José Osmar Ventura da Paz|Mazinho]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Ceará}} [[Ferroviário Atlético Clube (CE)|Ferroviário]]
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|VEN}} [[Rómulo Otero]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Minas Gerais}} [[Clube Atlético Mineiro|Atlético Mineiro]]
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Ricardo Oliveira]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Minas Gerais}} [[Clube Atlético Mineiro|Atlético Mineiro]]
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|PAR}} [[Ángel Romero (footballer)|Ángel Romero]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|São Paulo}} [[Sport Club Corinthians Paulista|Corinthians]]
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Valdívia (Brazilian footballer)|Valdívia]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|São Paulo}} [[São Paulo FC|São Paulo]]
|-
|align=left|{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Weverton Nunes Coelho|Weverton]]
|align=left|{{flagicon|Mato Grosso}} [[Cuiabá Esporte Clube|Cuiabá]]
|}
{{small|Source:CBF<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.cbf.com.br/competicoes/copa-brasil-masculino/artilharia/2018 |title=Artilharia |publisher=CBF |language=Portuguese}}</ref>}}

==References==
{{Reflist}}

{{Copa do Brasil}}
{{2018 in Brazilian football}}
{{2018 in South American football (CONMEBOL)}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Copa do Brasil 2018}}
[[Category:2018 Copa do Brasil| ]]
[[Category:Copa do Brasil seasons|2018]]
[[Category:2018 in Brazilian football]]
[[Category:2018 in domestic association football cups]]
